title: Science and Hand Mountains with Seeker and Uncle Grisco
summary: Seeker and Uncle Grisco spend an evening exploring the mysteries of the natural world. Using a magical model of the Earth, Seeker demonstrates how shifting continental plates create deep ocean trenches and massive mountain ranges. Their curiosity extends to the mechanics of humidifiers and the unique characteristics of pine trees. The session ends with a brief visit from a giant friend at the door before both friends retire to their respective homes for the night.

highlightQuote.text: Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.text: All right, going to turn on the stars, but not until you tuck in your toys. So please tuck in those short ice creams.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Well, they have to be over here in the orange.
Okay, good. They're all tucked in. All right.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Dad
transcript.text: And... stars!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's tell a story about Seeker and Uncle Grisco. Ding-dong.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who's there? said Uncle Grisco.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, you're supposed to be telling Seeker.
Who's there? said Seeker.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Uncle Grisco.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, Uncle Grisco, what scientific things have you been wondering about lately? said Seeker.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Humidifiers or lamps or mountains, which one of those would you pick?
And Seeker said, 'Oh, very interesting. I pick mountains.'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Okay, so then let's do some science about mountains.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Do you have a question about mountains? said Seeker.
No, I don't really, but let's do some science. And but before we do our science, Izzy has to tell us... Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, thanks, Izzy, said Seeker. And so Seeker and Uncle Grisco sat down to talk about where mountains come from. And Seeker made a magical model of the planet Earth that could change over time so that Uncle Grisco could see where mountains come from. And Seeker said, 'Look at this. On the surface of our planet, there are big moving pieces of rock called the continental plates. And we can pretend our hands are the continental plates. Now watch this. Supposing this was one continental plate with this, my right and my left hand, and then my right was another. Sometimes they move apart from each other. And when they move apart, there's kind of a gap. And that gap is where we get things like a deep ocean trench. Do you know about those, Uncle Grisco?'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: No.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: A deep ocean trench, like the Mariana Trench, is where the deep ocean sea creatures live. That's what happens when the plates come apart. But what happens do you think when they come together?
There is no trench.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, the gap between them closes, that's right, so there's no trench. But then they keep pushing together. What do you think will happen when they push together? Push... see what my hands are doing? Push...
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: There's a mountain!
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Yes! Some mountains form when the continental plates push together and they push way up in the air.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But what about... what about this mountain?

Hey, you made your own mountain, said Seeker. Look at that, Uncle Grisco, you made a hand mountain yourself. Now if you just pretend your hands were the rocks of the earth, then you can see how the rocks get pushed up to make tall mountains.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Well, I made a little mountain.
You did, yes.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Or I could make a heart.
Oh, you can make a heart with your hands? said Seeker. That's very interesting. I don't know how to do that. Can you show me?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Like this.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, like this. That's beautiful.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want to show this to my mom.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, you can show her that at breakfast time tomorrow. And Seeker said, 'Uncle Grisco, do you have any other science questions you'd like to ask tonight?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.
Well, maybe we could do one about humidifiers. Do you have a question about humidifiers?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Humidifiers, humidifiers... mm-hmm.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: What would you like to know about humidifiers?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm pretending this is a house while we're doing our story.

Oh yes, Seeker and Uncle Grisco are in there. That's Seeker's house. And it's cozy and warm and well-lit inside that house. And from the outside, it glows. And Seeker and Uncle Grisco are inside discussing interesting scientific topics such as humidifiers and mountains.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Or what about Christmas trees?
What about Christmas trees?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: What you know about them?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, said Seeker, Christmas trees are... pine trees. Do you know about pine trees? said Seeker.
Yeah.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Yeah. They smell nice and they have instead of big flat leaves, they have pointy needles. And they have a nice cone shape. And...
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I'm looking in the window of a house. There must be a mouse in the house. Oh, who is it? Who is it? Maybe I'll ding the door. Ding-dong.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, and Seeker said, 'Who's there?'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Isabella. I thought a little mouse lived in here.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, no, this is my house, said Seeker. And Uncle Grisco is here. Would you like to come in and talk about science with us?
No. No, I'm too big.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well you could talk with us from out there. You could just talk and we'll hear you through the window or the door.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm at the door, so Christmas trees we decorate.
Yes, we do decorate them at Christmas time, that's true. They can be very beautiful.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: I'm turning the stars in the direction that you're going.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thank you. And do you have any questions about Christmas trees? said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Christmas trees light up with lights.
That's true. But what would you like to know about them?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: That they make kind of little Christmas tree. That's what I have here.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Alright. Well, I think Seeker and Uncle Grisco are going to finish their conversation about science and they say, 'Well, it's time for bed so everyone should go home.' Seeker's already at his home, but Uncle Grisco says thank you for the interesting discussion and goes home to his mole house.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, is this the end of our story?
And this is the end of our story.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: And is this the end of our stars and stories?
It is, yes.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Then the houses disappear away. But don't turn off the light yet!

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Uncle Grisco
characters.description: A nomadic mole scientist and science teacher, this subterranean inventor resides in a home brimming with wondrous experiments, serving as a devoted confidant to the gopher folk. Within his laboratory of glowing elixirs and prismatic shadows, he fuses volcanic lore with the study of light to translate the planet's restless movements and the scarred history of cosmic craters. A wandering sage who maps both deep tectonic veins and celestial paths, he frequently emerges from his sprawling tunnels to share ancient botanical secrets and astronomical insights with his friend Seeker.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

places.name: Seeker's House
places.description: Perched at the edge of a mountain, this warm stone sanctuary—Seeker’s original residence and whimsical home shared with Uncle Crisco—has evolved from a secret vault into the purring, chaotic heart of a massive feline gala. Within its well-lit halls where Muppets gather for snacks and scientific discussions, lies a special hidden closet where Seeker brews an enchanted collection of potions that shimmer in every imaginable color.

places.name: Mariana Trench
places.description: A deep ocean trench mentioned by Seeker as a place where deep-sea creatures live.

places.name: Mole House
places.description: Uncle Grisco’s subterranean sanctum, a sprawling burrow of packed earth and reinforced timber, has been reimagined as a vibrant communal hub. Beneath its low, amber-lit ceilings, the scent of damp clay mingles with the ozone of a whirring robot that serves as both a metallic playmate and a rhythmic guide. Here, amidst the clatter of clockwork and repurposed scrap, the group gathers to master intricate new dances, their shadows flickering against the cavern walls in a spirited fusion of technology and grace.
